celebration: THE INFLECTION UNLOCK — for the first time the cast DOES things. Add -ing and the word is happening now (run → running); add -ed and it already happened (play → played); add -s or -es and there are more of them. Every base word in the whole program can suddenly bend — the readable inventory multiplies overnight.

songs.title: Running, Jumping, Singing!
songs.lyrics:
[Intro]
(-ing… -ed… -s…)
Add an ending — the word grows!

[Hook]
Run… RUNNING! (now! now!)
Jump… JUMPING! (now! now!)
Sing… SINGING! (right now!)
Add -ing — it's happening NOW!

[Verse 2]
Play… PLAYED! (all done!)
Look… LOOKED! (all done!)
Jump… JUMPED! (all done!)
Add -ed — it already happened!

[Verse 3]
One cat… two CATS!
One dog… two DOGS!
One dish… two DISHES!
Add -s, add -es — now there's MORE!

[Bridge]
A potato?! (NO!)
P-p-potato! No ending!
(-ing… -ed… -s…)

[Outro]
running… played… wishes…
Every word can grow! (grow! grow!)
(-ing… shhh…)
songs.sunoStyle: dark trap, 68 bpm, heavy 808 bass, sparse hi-hats, deep whisper-rap verses, kids choir chant on hook, playful spooky, minimal, clean vocals, nursery trap
songs.sunoNotes: 🎉 THE MULTIPLIER WEEK — the transformation IS the beat: hit the 808 on each ending (run→RUNNING). Say whole words (never syllable-split; the base word snaps to its inflected form). -ed says three ways by ear (played /d/, looked /t/, landed /ɪd/) — model all three, don't drill the rule. Verse 3 is plural -s/-es. Potato bridge intact — potato has no ending.

songs.title: Running!
songs.lyrics:
[Intro]
(We can read. We can read!)

[Hook]
Running! Running!
The cast is running!
Who is running? (who? who?)
All of us — running!

[Verse 2]
The dog is running.
The cat is jumping.
The duck is swimming! (splash! splash!)
Bee is singing.

[Verse 3]
Sam played and played.
Sheep is reading.
The fish wishes and wishes! (wish! wish!)
Everybody is doing something!

[Outro]
running… jumping… reading… (shhh…)
But who is resting?!
(POTATO!)
Running! (the whole cast can move!)
songs.sunoStyle: dark trap, 68 bpm, heavy 808 bass, sparse hi-hats, deep whisper-rap verses, kids choir chant on hook, playful spooky, minimal, clean vocals, nursery trap
songs.sunoNotes: Frame: 'Running!' — the whole cast finally in MOTION, each doing a verb. This is the first song of real connected action, not just naming. Let each member's line land on their verb (dog running, cat jumping, duck swimming). Whisper the outro; the one cast member NOT moving is the potato, resting — the class shouts it.

spreads.artNote: A blur of joyful motion — legs and dust and speed lines, the whole cast about to charge in. One word, all energy. The book that finally MOVES.
The Dog — brown, cast member since Week 12 — flat-out sprinting, ears streaming back, tongue out, pure joy. '-ing' = happening now.
The Cat mid-leap, all four legs off the ground, a small startled bird flushing up ahead.
The Duck powering across dark water, a bright V-wake behind it, delighted.
Bee hovering with musical notes rising from it, eyes shut in song.
Sam surrounded by scattered toys, mid-play, grinning — 'played' twice, the past tense, already done and done again.
Sheep curled with an open book, one hoof on the page, absorbed. The friend who could not read its own name once, now reading.
The Fish (from Week 27!) back, eyes shut, bubbles rising like wishes — 'wishes', the -es ending. A callback to the very first pattern book.
Everyone is in motion — except, propped on a tiny deckchair with sunglasses, a single potato, resting. No noun printed — the class SHOUTS it. The only still thing on a page full of movement.

extras: 🎉 THE MULTIPLIER: this is the biggest true unlock of the program. It is not four new words — it is a machine that bends every word the class already owns. Take a base word from the 1,100-word wall, add -ing (happening now), -ed (already happened), or -s/-es (more than one) and read the new word. Suddenly the readable inventory is not 1,100 words — it is thousands.

-ed BY EAR (three sounds, no rule drilled): played says /d/, looked says /t/, landed says /ɪd/. Sort a stack of -ed words into three sound-boxes by listening — the child discovers the three voices of -ed without a single rule written down.

THE DOUBLING RULE, DRAMATIZED not drilled: run → running (the n doubles to keep the vowel short). Show it with the moveable alphabet — slide a second n in — as a visible event, not a worksheet. hop→hopping, dig→digging.

Pattern-card tracing (pattern mode): the base word in black, the ending (-ing/-ed/-s/-es) in red — trace the ending on and watch the word change job. This is the last new material of the program before graduation.

teacherFocus: 🎉 THE MULTIPLIER WEEK. Everything before this taught new spellings; this teaches word-BENDING, and it multiplies the whole inventory. Three endings: -ing (happening now: run→running), -ed (already happened: play→played), -s/-es (more than one: cat→cats, dish→dishes). Teach -ed by EAR — played /d/, looked /t/, landed /ɪd/ — sort into three sound-boxes, no rule written. Dramatize the doubling rule with the moveable alphabet (slide a second n into run→running) rather than a worksheet. This is the last new content before graduation: after today, the cast can finally be described DOING, and the class can read real connected stories.
